sn11.22:1.3: “Once, monks, a certain yakkha—ugly & misshapen—sat down on Sakka the deva-king’s seat.
sn11.22:1.4: Then the devas of the Thirty-three were indignant, annoyed, & complained:
sn11.22:1.5: ‘Isn’t it amazing, good sirs! Isn’t it astounding!
sn11.22:1.6: This yakkha—ugly & misshapen—has sat down on Sakka’s seat!’
sn11.22:1.7: But the more the devas of the Thirty-three were indignant, annoyed, & complained, the more well-formed that yakkha became, the more good-looking & inspiring.
sn11.22:2.1: “Then the devas of the Thirty-three went to Sakka the deva-king and, on arrival, said to him,
sn11.22:2.2: ‘Just now, dear sir, a certain yakkha—ugly & misshapen—sat down on your seat.
sn11.22:2.3: The devas of the Thirty-three were indignant, annoyed, & complained:
sn11.22:2.4: “Isn’t it amazing, good sirs! Isn’t it astounding!
sn11.22:2.5: This yakkha—ugly & misshapen—has sat down on Sakka’s seat!”
sn11.22:2.6: But the more the devas of the Thirty-three were indignant, annoyed, & complained, the more well-formed that yakkha became, the more good-looking & inspiring.’
sn11.22:2.7: “‘Then, dear sirs, he must be an anger-eating yakkha.’
sn11.22:3.1: “So Sakka the deva-king approached the anger-eating yakkha and, on arrival, arranging his upper robe over one shoulder & kneeling with one knee on the ground, raised his hands palm-to-palm over his heart toward the anger-eating yakkha and announced his name three times, ‘I, dear sir, am Sakka the deva-king!
sn11.22:3.2: I, dear sir, am Sakka the deva-king!
sn11.22:3.3: I, dear sir, am Sakka the deva-king!’1 The more Sakka the deva-king announced his name, the uglier & more misshapen the yakkha became.
sn11.22:3.4: Then, having become uglier & more misshapen, he disappeared right there.
sn11.22:3.5: “Then Sakka the deva-king, sitting down on his seat & conciliating the devas of the Thirthy-three, on that occasion recited these verses:
sn11.22:4.2: nor easily led into a whirl.
sn11.22:4.3: I don’t get angry for long.
sn11.22:5.1: When angry, I don’t speak harshly
sn11.22:5.3: I keep myself well under control
sn11.22:5.4: with an eye to my own good.’”
